name: tripwire-fixtures-conventions
trigger: When working in the Apache Superset fork (cdugo/superset) on the tripwire/demo branch, remediating advisories under fixtures/tripwire/{web-dashboard,api-gateway,etl-worker,report-api}.
body:
Build, install, and CI conventions for the Tripwire demo fixtures in https://github.com/cdugo/superset on branch `tripwire/demo`.

FIXTURE LAYOUT
All four fixtures live under `fixtures/tripwire/`:
  - web-dashboard/  (npm, React/TypeScript SPA)
  - api-gateway/    (npm, Node/Express)
  - etl-worker/     (PyPI, Celery worker)
  - report-api/     (PyPI, Flask service)

NPM CONVENTIONS (web-dashboard, api-gateway)
From within the fixture directory:
  - Install:   `npm ci --ignore-scripts --no-audit --no-fund`
  - Build:     `npm run build`  (web-dashboard runs `tsc --noEmit`; api-gateway runs `tsc -p tsconfig.json`)
  - Lint/test: scripts exist but are stubbed (`echo skip-*`); the only meaningful check is the build.
Lockfile is `package-lock.json` and IS committed. Always re-run `npm install` after changing `package.json` so the lockfile resolves with the new versions; commit the regenerated lockfile.
Transitive remediations: use the npm `overrides` field in the root `package.json` rather than editing a direct dependency you do not own (e.g. force `tough-cookie` to a safe version above what the parent declares).

PYPI CONVENTIONS (etl-worker, report-api)
From within the fixture directory:
  - Install:   `pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt`
  - Build/import-check (etl-worker):  `python -c "from workers.etl import render_summary; print(render_summary('demo', 1, 1.0))"`
  - Build/import-check (report-api):  `python -c "from app import create_app; app = create_app(); print(list(app.url_map.iter_rules()))"`
Dev-only deps live in `requirements-dev.txt` (`-r requirements.txt` plus pytest etc.); the poller only scans `requirements.txt`. Do not move runtime deps into the dev file to evade the scanner.
Transitive remediations: use a `constraints.txt` referenced from a pip install command, or pin the transitive directly in `requirements.txt` with a comment naming the parent that pulled it.

CI WORKFLOW
`.github/workflows/tripwire-fixtures.yml` runs four parallel jobs (one per manifest) on PRs that touch `fixtures/tripwire/**` or the workflow file itself. Each job has a 5-minute timeout. The PR must show all four jobs green before remediation is considered complete; Autofix will iterate.

BASE BRANCH
All remediation PRs target `tripwire/demo`, not `master`. Branch from `tripwire/demo` and open the PR against `tripwire/demo`.

WHAT NOT TO TOUCH
Anything outside `fixtures/tripwire/**` and `.github/workflows/tripwire-fixtures.yml` is real Apache Superset code; do not modify it as part of a remediation. If you find yourself wanting to, stop and post a comment on the tracking issue.
